case_studies.title: Amazon's TAM Expansion Story

thesis.expansion: Extended to all retail, then cloud, advertising, streaming
thesis.moat_evolution: Distribution network, Prime membership, data flywheel
thesis.result: From books to $500B+ revenue across multiple massive TAMs

lessons: Initial TAM can dramatically underestimate true opportunity
Platform companies can expand into adjacent markets
Reinvesting all profits into growth can create enormous value

case_studies.title: Netflix's Disruption of Entertainment

phases.phase_1: Low-end disruption of video rental (DVD by mail)

lessons: Disruption often starts in segments incumbents ignore

case_studies.title: Peter Lynch's Dunkin' Donuts

thesis.observation: Noticed coffee was great, stores always busy

lessons: Best ideas can come from everyday observations
Simple businesses can be great investments
Know what you own - actually use the product

case_studies.title: Philip Fisher's Motorola Investment

thesis.growth_drivers: R&D culture, management quality, market expansion
thesis.holding_period: Held for decades through multiple technology cycles

lessons: Quality of management determines long-term outcomes
Primary research provides edge over desk analysis
